creator: pfg@FreeBSD.org
text:
Hi Thibault;

If it's your first port then it's certainly a brave *good* effort.
FreeCAD is/was not easy.

(In reply to Thibault Jouan from comment #5)
>  Just to be sure, What do you mean by "generally building"?

I just means it builds.. although I am not happy about how it was achieved.
I am not a CMake expert.


>  I worked with 0.16 for a few reasons, at the time I started this it
> seemed the only "official" release (tagged) and also because it was
> available under Debian unstable linux and I could compare how program
> was working.

Upstream is careful to note that this is under development and not ready for general use. Working with the upstream version has better chance of working on the long run. I looked at the patches in this port and they, or a variation of them, are already in the upstream version.


>  About taking over maintaining the port, I'm OK to do it, but be
> warned I lack experience for this for this here and will most likely
> require guidance to finish initial submission, and maybe in the future
> (I guess freebsd-ports@freebsd.org is the right place to ask for help
> with general questions).

Just wait for the port to be committed, once you have an update (and that is easy since the port is a moving target), feel free to set yourself as maintainer.
